aluminium-foil-trays.notes: RNY 'aluminium-cans' is the closest match; foil trays follow similar kerbside rules — typically recyclable if clean

animal-waste.notes: UNMAPPED — RNY has no specific page for animal waste; general waste (red bin) is the standard advice

bagged-recyclables.notes: UNMAPPED — describes presentation, not material; RNY advice is to empty bags and recycle contents separately; bags contaminate sorting machinery

bagged-rubbish.notes: UNMAPPED — describes presentation, not material; general waste (red bin) is assumed; RNY has no page for this presentation type

batteries-electronics.notes: RNY uses separate slugs 'batteries-single-use', 'lead-acid-batteries', and 'electrical' / 'electrical-battery-operated'; 'batteries-single-use' is primary; electronics may also map to 'electrical-battery-operated'

building-materials.notes: RNY uses 'demolition' for building / demolition waste including timber, bricks, plaster

bulky-items.notes: RNY 'furniture' is the closest for large bulky household items; also see 'whitegoods' and 'mattresses' for specific bulky items

cardboard.notes: RNY combines paper and cardboard under 'paper-cardboard'

cd-dvd-cases.notes: RNY uses 'cds-dvds' covering discs and their cases

ceramics.notes: Mapped to 'demolition' as closest RNY category for hard inorganic waste; ceramics cannot go in recycling (contaminate glass); check council hard waste or skip bin options

citrus-aromatics.notes: Citrus peel and aromatic plant waste is typically accepted in green organics bin; mapped to 'garden-organics' as closest RNY page; food scraps bin also relevant in some councils

clothing-textiles.notes: RNY uses 'clothing' for clothing and textiles

coat-hangers.notes: Wire/metal coat hangers map to 'scrap-metals'; plastic hangers are general waste; donation preferred where possible — LLM should advise based on material type

coffee-cups-pods.notes: RNY uses 'coffee-cups' for takeaway cups; coffee pods/capsules have separate RNY slug 'coffee-capsules' — split in v2 if classifier is separated

compostable-liners.notes: Certified compostable bin liners (AS4736) are accepted in FOGO/green organics bins in most councils; mapped to 'garden-organics' as closest RNY page; LLM should confirm council acceptance and AS4736 certification requirement

compostable-packaging.notes: Certified compostable packaging may be accepted in FOGO/green organics in some councils; mapped to 'garden-organics' as closest RNY page; acceptance varies widely — LLM must confirm council-specific rules and certification status

crockery.notes: Mapped to 'demolition' as closest RNY category for ceramic/hard inorganic waste; crockery cannot go in recycling (contaminates glass); council hard waste collection or charity donation if intact

dairy-eggshells.notes: Dairy packaging and eggshells are organic food waste; mapped to RNY 'food' page for general food disposal advice

dead-animals.notes: UNMAPPED — RNY has no page for dead animals; council pickup or vet disposal required; general waste as last resort

disposable-cutlery-plates.notes: Rigid plastic disposable plates map to 'plastic-containers' if clean; disposable cutlery and mixed-material items are general waste; LLM should advise based on material type and cleanliness

food-leftovers.notes: RNY 'food' page covers food scraps and leftovers; FOGO or green organics bin where available

food-packaging.notes: Broad category; mapped to 'plastic-containers' as most common food packaging material; LLM should refine by specific material (glass, carton, soft plastic, paper) for accurate advice

fruit-vegetable-scraps.notes: RNY 'food' covers fruit and vegetable scraps; FOGO or green organics bin where available

garden-waste.notes: Direct match in concept; RNY uses 'garden-organics' for garden clippings, grass, leaves

gas-bottles-extinguishers.notes: RNY uses 'gas-bottles'; fire extinguishers are similar hazardous pressurised containers — same disposal advice applies; special drop-off required

general-rubbish.notes: General waste — no specific RNY item page; LLM uses training data; red bin (general waste) is always the answer for this category

glass-bottles-jars.notes: RNY uses 'glass-containers' for glass bottles and jars

glass-mirrors.notes: Mapped to 'demolition' as closest RNY category for hard waste; mirror glass cannot be recycled with container glass (different composition and coatings); hard waste collection or general waste

grains-cereals.notes: Grains and cereals are organic food waste; mapped to RNY 'food' page

hair.notes: UNMAPPED — RNY has no page for hair; general waste (red bin)

hazardous-chemicals.notes: RNY 'chemicals' covers household hazardous chemicals; also see 'pool-chemicals' for pool-specific chemicals; requires special drop-off

hessian-bags.notes: Hessian/jute bags are a natural fibre textile; mapped to 'clothing' as closest RNY textile category; donation or reuse preferred if in good condition; worn-out hessian to general waste

hot-ashes.notes: UNMAPPED — RNY has no page for hot ashes; must be fully cooled before disposal; general waste once cold

large-logs-stumps.notes: Mapped to 'garden-organics' but councils have size limits; large logs and stumps typically require hard waste or green waste collection — LLM should note size restrictions

liquids.notes: Mapped to 'chemicals' as primary RNY page for liquid disposal; specific liquids have better matches: cooking oil → 'cooking-oil', motor oil → 'motor-oil'; LLM must refine by liquid type — never advise pouring down drain

meat-seafood-bones.notes: Meat, seafood, and bones are organic food waste; mapped to RNY 'food' page; FOGO bins where available

metal-cans-tins.notes: RNY uses 'steel-cans' for metal cans and tins; aluminium cans also map to 'aluminium-cans' but 'steel-cans' is the primary kerbside recyclable

motor-oil-containers.notes: RNY 'motor-oil' covers motor oil and its containers; special drop-off required via DriveZero or similar program

nappies.notes: Mapped to RNY 'food' as closest organic waste category; standard disposable nappies are general waste (red bin); some councils accept certified compostable nappies in FOGO — LLM should advise checking council rules

non-approved-liners.notes: UNMAPPED — context-specific: bin liners that are not council-approved compostable liners; general waste (red bin)

oil-paints.notes: RNY 'paint' covers all paint types including oil-based; special drop-off required via PaintBack program

paper.notes: RNY combines paper and cardboard under 'paper-cardboard'

plant-pots.notes: Plastic plant pots mapped to 'plastic-containers'; acceptance varies by council — many do not accept plant pots in kerbside recycling due to soil contamination and dark plastic type; check local garden centre take-back schemes

plastic-bags-soft-plastic.notes: RNY uses 'soft-plastics' for plastic bags and soft packaging; REDcycle program paused but alternatives exist at some supermarkets

plastic-bottles-containers.notes: RNY uses 'plastic-containers' for rigid plastic bottles and containers

plastic-coated-paper.notes: Mapped to 'paper-cardboard' for RNY guidance context; however plastic-coated paper (e.g. glossy paper, thermal paper, laminated) is generally NOT recyclable kerbside — LLM must clarify that coating prevents recycling and advise general waste

plastic-lids.notes: Mapped to 'plastic-containers'; lids should be screwed back onto bottles before recycling per most council advice

plastic-lined-cardboard.notes: RNY 'cartons' covers liquid paperboard (Tetrapak, milk cartons) which are cardboard with plastic/foil lining; some councils accept kerbside

plastic-strapping.notes: Plastic strapping is a type of soft plastic; mapped to 'soft-plastics' — cannot be recycled kerbside, requires soft plastics drop-off

plastic-tubes.notes: Plastic tubes (toothpaste tubes, hand cream, etc.) mapped to 'plastic-containers'; acceptance varies — rigid HDPE/PP tubes yes, soft multi-layer tubes often general waste

polystyrene-foam.notes: RNY uses 'polystyrene' for polystyrene foam (EPS); generally not accepted kerbside; special drop-off or general waste

reusable-containers.notes: Mapped to 'plastic-containers' for end-of-life disposal guidance; reusable containers should first be donated or reused; if disposal needed, material-specific pages apply (plastic, glass, metal)

rocks-soil.notes: UNMAPPED — RNY has no page for rocks and soil; cannot go in any kerbside bin; requires hard waste collection or skip bin

scrap-metal.notes: RNY uses 'scrap-metals' for scrap metal; typically requires hard waste collection or metal recycler drop-off

seafood-shells.notes: Seafood shells (oyster, mussel, prawn) are organic material; mapped to RNY 'food' page; accepted in FOGO bins in many councils

shredded-laminated-paper.notes: Mapped to 'paper-cardboard' for context; shredded paper clogs sorting machinery — some councils accept in sealed clear bag in recycling; laminated paper is NOT recyclable (general waste); LLM must distinguish between shredded and laminated paper

steel-pots-pans.notes: Steel pots and pans are scrap metal; too large for kerbside recycling bin; requires hard waste collection or metal recycler

syringes-sharps.notes: UNMAPPED — RNY has no page for sharps; must use sharps disposal containers and council sharps programs; never in any kerbside bin

tea-coffee-grounds.notes: Tea leaves and coffee grounds are organic food waste; mapped to RNY 'food' page; accepted in FOGO or green organics bins

teabags.notes: Teabags are organic food waste; most modern teabags contain plastic mesh — check brand; mapped to RNY 'food' for FOGO guidance

tissues-paper-towel.notes: Mapped to 'paper-cardboard' for context; used tissues and paper towels are NOT recyclable (contamination, short fibres) — general waste; clean/unused paper towels could theoretically be composted in FOGO but this is uncommon advice

toothpaste-toothbrushes.notes: Mapped to 'plastic-containers' for guidance context; toothpaste tubes are multi-layer (not recyclable kerbside); plastic toothbrushes are general waste; Colgate recycling program via TerraCycle at some pharmacies — LLM should mention specialty recycling option

vacuum-dust.notes: UNMAPPED — RNY has no page for vacuum dust; general waste (red bin); contains mixed fine materials including fibres and soil

waxed-cardboard.notes: Mapped to 'paper-cardboard' for guidance context; waxed cardboard is NOT accepted in kerbside recycling (wax coating prevents pulping) — general waste; LLM must clarify the wax coating exception to normal cardboard recycling

wet-wipes.notes: UNMAPPED — RNY has no page for wet wipes; general waste (red bin); wet wipes contain plastic fibres and must never be flushed

wooden-utensils.notes: Untreated wooden utensils can go in green organics bin in many councils; treated/painted wood is general waste; mapped to 'garden-organics' with caveat about treatment
